Knowing has a few different models

==Tripartite model of knowledge==
[[File:Tripartite Knowing.svg|thumb|knowledge]]
Most recent one proposed by [[Immanuelle]]. It is a modification of Vervaeke's theory of wisdom. 

It merges [[Participatory knowing]] and [[Perspectival knowing]] on the basis that perspectival is more of a mundane subset of participatory than its own thing. This is because Immanuelle believes Vervaeke was mistaken in deriving perspectival knowing first, and then needing to accomodade the elements of participatory knowing not present in it 

# [[Alpha]] [[Phenomenological knowing]]
# [[Lambda]] [[Procedural knowing]]
# [[Omega]] [[Propositional knowing]]
Credit for the first group to propose merging them goes to the [[Mark of Wisdom]] discord server.

==Vervaeke's model of knowledge==

[[File:Knowing.svg|thumb|Relation between the kinds of knowing]]Knowing has a few levels. Each one is dependent on all the last ones<ref>https://www.meaningcrisis.co/ep-29-awakening-from-the-meaning-crisis-getting-to-the-depths-of-relevance-realization/</ref>

# [[Participatory knowing]] == [[Relevance realization]], or [[self organizing criticality]] forming [[small world networks]]
# [[Perspectival knowing]]
# [[Procedural knowing]]
# [[Propositional knowing]]
For more info and future research on this page see this article<ref>https://www.snsociety.org/spirituality-and-responding-to-the-meaning-crisis-part-1-the-meaning-that-was-lost/</ref>

{{Quote|It’s important to distinguish different kinds of knowledge:

* Propositional knowledge (episteme): For example, knowing that energy and mass are equivalent or that Paris is the capital of France.
* Procedural knowledge (techne): For example, knowing how to compose a symphony or how to throw a baseball perfectly. Often people who are capable of doing such things are unable to put this knowledge into a list of propositions. Moreover, one can’t learn how to be an expert pitcher by reading textbooks on baseball.
* Perspectival knowledge (noesis): For example, knowing what it’s like to see Earth from the Moon or what it is like to be a human living in the post-World War Two Germany..
* Participatory knowledge (gnosis): knowing by sharing a fundamental identity with something, a knowing by becoming, which also comes with a certain degree of imitation and internalisation. It’s the knowing you get by binding your identity to something and letting your identity being transformed in conjunction with how that thing is transforming. For example, knowing how to coordinate with your team to win the NBA Championship or how to be a parent by participating in parenthood or how you know a person you love. You don’t just have beliefs about a person you love, or some particular skill that allows you to “work” with them. You know them by how differently you know yourself and the world in knowing them.
 
|[[awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com]]<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20210307142111/https://awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com/knowledge</ref>}}[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Knowledge]]== Excerpt ==
''Participatory knowing'' refers to knowing how to act in the “agent-arena” environment. It is simultaneously one of the most basic and most profound kinds of knowing. One way to think about participatory knowledge is to consider the difference between being in a state of confusion versus a state of flow. Flow is when you are in a groove and feel a natural “dance” between your actions and the environment, and an example of participatory knowledge.<ref name=":0">https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/blog/theory-knowledge/202101/john-vervaeke-s-brilliant-4p3r-metatheory-cognition</ref>

''Perspectival knowing'' refers to knowing via embodied perception. It consists of seeing the world and one’s place in it via a specific point of view, and understanding (or not) the key aspects of a situation.<ref name=":0" />

''Procedural knowing'' means knowing how to do something. This can be very complicated, like knowing how to complete surgery or something simple like tying one’s shoe.<ref name=":0" />

Finally, there is ''propositional knowing'', which is knowing that something is true. This kind of knowing is closely tied to language and justification (see here for more on the 4 Ps and their implications for modern society).<ref name=":0" />

== See Also ==

* [[Leonhart's Ethics]]

== References ==
<references />
[[Category:Knowledge]]
[[sn:Knowledge]]
[[Category:Pages]]
